description_long: This is a robust hammer featuring a solid, dark metallic head and a medium-length wooden handle. The head is cylindrical with a flat striking face on one end and a rounded, ball-peen shape on the opposite side, making it suitable for metalworking and shaping tasks. The handle is slightly curved, crafted from wood with a visibly worn, textured surface, indicating frequent use. The metal head is securely fitted to the handle, showing some surface wear and minor discoloration, but no major damage. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements. This hammer would commonly be found in urban garages, workshops, or construction sites.
description: A sturdy hammer with a worn wooden handle and a dark, cylindrical metal head featuring a flat and a rounded end. The tool shows signs of use and is designed for striking and shaping tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the cylindrical metal head with both flat and rounded ends visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the width of the metal head and the curvature of the handle, with the flat striking face facing forward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's grain and the rounded ball-peen end of the head more prominently.
description_view_3: Top-down view focusing on the attachment point between the handle and head, with both ends of the head visible.
attribute_handle_type: curved wooden handle with a textured, worn surface and no additional grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: ball-peen hammer head with one flat striking face and one rounded, ball-shaped peen
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible surface wear and discoloration on both the handle and head, but no major damage
